TextENGNew: It took me a few moments to even grasp what Rena had just said.
Mion clicked her tongue and gave a small shake of her head.
Before Satoshi-kun disappeared, he told me. He said someone was watching him. Following him. Even coming into his house. He told me they stood by his pillow while he slept and looked down at him.
changeReason: Dialogue quotes removed despite Japanese text being dialogue

TextENGNew: ...Stop it, Rena.

changeReason: Dialogue quotes removed despite Japanese text being dialogue

TextENGNew: ...I told you to stop...
changeReason: Dialogue quotes removed despite Japanese text being dialogue

TextENGNew: Oyashiro-sama is Hinamizawa's guardian deity. ...He absolutely never forgives anyone who tries to abandon Hinamizawa and run away. I apologized for that! But Satoshi-kun surely didn't.
changeReason: Dialogue quotes removed despite Japanese text being dialogue

TextENGNew: That's why! It has to be Oyashiro-sama's curse!!

TextENGNew: ...Oyashiro-sama's... curse...?

TextENGNew: That's just a lie cooked up by the village bigwigs who don't want to admit it's a curse!
Whether you believe in Oyashiro-sama's curse or not, it's real, and—

It wasn't a playful tap.
I told you to cut it out already, didn't I?! Even I lose my temper eventually!!
changeReason: Dialogue quotes removed despite Japanese text being dialogue

TextENGNew: It was more than just eventually getting angry... unmistakable, bitter anger was written all over Mion's face.
...Rena, after being hit, wore a sullen look for a while.
...But perhaps the cicadas' cries cooled her heated heart, because after a while her usual calm expression returned...
We reached the usual fork where we part ways with Mion.
...No one had spoken a word on the way here.
...Only the cicadas' cries rang relentlessly, gnawing at my ears.
Normally we'd say goodbye to Mion here, and it would be just Rena and me.
Hey, Kei-chan. Remember when I promised to lend you that manga? If you want, why don't you stop by?
changeReason: Dialogue quotes removed despite Japanese text being dialogue

TextENGNew: ...Huh?
Did we make a promise like that?

TextENGNew: After a while, Mion finally spoke.

TextENGNew:

TextENGNew: ........No, that's not even the issue.

TextENGNew:
...Why does everyone get so worked up about it...?
(Idiot! Drop it, Maebara Keiichi!!)

What... happened to Satoshi?

TextENGNew: Mion stayed silent for a long time, but... maybe she couldn't take it anymore, because she finally opened her mouth, timidly...
Have you ever heard about Oyashiro-sama's curse?
...Just a little.

TextENGNew: ...I know the rough idea.

TextENGNew: I only overheard one of my classmates talking about it.
It seems Oyashiro-sama is Hinamizawa's guardian deity, enshrined at that shrine where we had the barbecue today.

...So, a few years ago, there was a dam project that would have submerged Hinamizawa... and around that time, some construction people died or something, and it seems that got taken as Oyashiro-sama's curse.
...That's the story.
About Satoshi's dead parents... they were on the side pushing for the dam.

TextENGNew:

Even though they were villagers, there were supporters too...?

TextENGNew: A dam plan that would drown all of Hinamizawa.
...I'd heard the whole village united to fight that terrifying plan, but... it's hard to believe there were villagers who supported it.

It wasn't like everyone opposed the dam plan.
...And those parents were one of the leading figures on the pro-dam side.

changeReason: Dialogue quotes removed despite Japanese text being dialogue

TextENGNew: There may have been a few exceptions, but basically, Hinamizawa was not a wealthy village.
It wasn't about pride in land passed down from their forefathers.
So it wasn't strange that some people wanted money to start a new life.
...Even so, it was the kind of situation where saying that out loud was hard.
Knowing that, the ones who stood up as representatives for people like that... just happened to be Satoko and Satoshi's parents.
In that sense, you could say Satoko and Satoshi's parents were very brave.
...They chose to play the villain for poor people who couldn't raise their voices.

TextENGNew:

died in an accident on the day of the festival for Oyashiro-sama.

TextENGNew: ...I remembered what the director told me today.

....................Well... yeah.

none of that is pleasant to hear.

TextENGNew: With just those words, Mion waved and walked away.
...I watched her retreating figure in a daze for a moment, then started walking myself.
............Satoko's older brother, Satoshi.
Had he run away from home?
...Had the curse claimed him, leaving no trace behind?
............Either way, all of it was things I'd have been better off not asking about...
changeReason: Translation substantially rewritten with very different wording

TextENGNew: ...The biting chill in the air pressed me hard toward self-reflection.
............Even though I wished for Satoko's smile,
I'd caved to curiosity so easily, blundering half for fun into the darkness of her past... ...I was pathetic.
I wanted today to be exactly the same as yesterday.
I wanted tomorrow to be just as fun as today.
......So why did I have to go and do something like this...?
Yesterday, we'd had a blast at the baseball tournament.
And today, we'd gotten all fired up at the victory barbecue.
It had been so much fun.
......And yet, I went out of my way to...@k ...to do something that practically erased all those good times.
......Starting tomorrow, I'd be more careful.
I'd make sure I never did anything this unpleasant again.
...Like hell I'd let these happy days end over one careless slip-up today...
But no matter how much I told myself I'd be more careful tomorrow,
......I couldn't shake the ominous feeling that what just happened had already put an end to those happy days.
...Get a grip, Keiichi Maebara.
...Fate turns on impossibly massive gears, doesn't it?
Something that trivial just now...@k ......there's no way it could radically change my life starting tomorrow. Common sense wouldn't allow it.
......Even if I understood that rationally.
......Until I could confirm that tomorrow would be the same kind of fun day I'd had until now, ...this unease in my chest wasn't going away.
......I'm being weird. ......That whole exchange with everyone just now... I'm reading way too much into it...
changeReason: Dialogue quotes removed despite Japanese text being dialogue

TextENGNew: I wanted tomorrow to come already.
And I wanted it to show me just how groundless these petty worries of mine really were.
......The night stretched on, endlessly.
